title: phaseolus acutifolius var. acutifolius
intro: Phaseolus acutifolius var. acutifolius, also known as the tepary bean, is a type of wild bean plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The tepary bean has been reported to have numerous health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and lowering blood sugar levels. The herbal preparations of Phaseolus acutifolius var. acutifolius include dried bean pods, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
